title: Tripleurospermum rosellum
taxonomy: Tripleurospermum rosellum is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Tripleurospermum, which is a group of flowering plants. The species is further categorized as a member of the order Asterales. Tripleurospermum rosellum is also known as small-flowered hawk's beard, and its classification reflects its botanical characteristics. The plant's classification is based on its morphological features and genetic relationships with other related species.

intro: Tripleurospermum rosellum, also known as lesser meadow rue or meadow cress, is an annual or biennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tripleurospermum rosellum include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical preparations for its skin-soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
